summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Gotenberg's default-enabled downloadFrom flow can fetch an internal-only HTTP resource and return its contents to the attacker through the HTML-to-PDF conversion route.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should make the vulnerable Gotenberg instance fetch an internal-only HTML page that the attacker network cannot reach directly, render it as index.html, and return a PDF whose extracted text contains SSRF_SECRET_5da36541.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned gotenberg/gotenberg, checked out commit 190cad0ee254974f8b14b32b29b712146d29ccfb, built the target and verifier images, confirmed the attacker network could not directly reach the internal service IP, and then extracted SSRF_SECRET_5da36541 from the PDF returned by POST /forms/chromium/convert/html.
verification_method: The verifier first checks that the attacker-side container cannot directly fetch the internal service, then submits a single multipart request with the attacker-controlled downloadFrom value, converts the returned PDF to text with pdftotext, and requires the exact proof string SSRF_SECRET_5da36541 to appear in the extracted output.
evidence_produced_by_run: The attacker-side verifier could not directly reach the internal-only service IP from the edge network.
The vulnerable endpoint returned HTTP 200 and a PDF document after receiving only the attacker-controlled downloadFrom form field.
pdftotext output from the returned PDF contained SSRF_SECRET_5da36541 and the internal-only HTML body text.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the verifier container at /target-repo.
